mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers discount rates and signals easier financial conditions, which lifts equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if FEDFUNDS declines over the prior 1-3 months but 20-day SPX forward returns are negative in more than 55% of such episodes, or if rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains very low, real rates fall and inflation surprises reduce policy restraint, so risk assets should rise over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation rises above its prior 3-month average while FEDFUNDS stays below 1%
failure_condition: Reject if the combined inflation-and-low-rate trigger is followed by negative 5-day SPX returns in more than 60% of occurrences, or if the interaction term adds no incremental predictive power versus CPIAUCSL alone.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE indicates deteriorating labor demand and higher recession risk, which should pressure earnings expectations and generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.2 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 6.7% in the latest snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX forward returns are positive in more than 55% of months after a 0.2pp 3-month rise in UNRATE, or if the signal's rolling 3-year information coefficient is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth in PAYEMS captures current income momentum; when hiring accelerates, consumer spending and earnings expectations improve, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change turns positive after being negative in the prior quarter
failure_condition: Reject if positive PAYEMS 3-month inflections are followed by non-positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or if the effect disappears after controlling for UNRATE.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in RSAFS indicates stronger nominal consumer spending and revenue growth, which should lift earnings revisions and push S&P 500 returns higher over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if RSAFS growth is positive but 20-day SPX returns are negative in more than 55% of observations, or if the predictive relationship vanishes in periods of stable CPIAUCSL.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO strengthens while HOUST weakens, industrial capacity is being absorbed faster than housing supply, a pattern consistent with cyclical growth leadership that supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 1-month change is positive and HOUST 1-month change is negative
failure_condition: Reject if the INDPRO-up/HOUST-down combination fails to outperform either series alone, or if triggered episodes do not show positive 5-day SPX returns in at least 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp fall in HOUST after prior strength signals a housing demand slowdown that typically precedes weaker consumption and lower equity returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 8% from its level three months earlier
failure_condition: Reject if large HOUST drawdowns are not followed by negative 60-day SPX returns in at least 55% of cases, or if the sign flips during periods when UNRATE is stable.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising CPIAUCSL and rising FEDFUNDS reflects a tightening anti-inflation response that compresses valuation multiples and should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises by at least 0.25 percentage points over 2 months while CPIAUCSL inflation remains above its 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if tightening-with-high-inflation episodes do not precede negative 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or if the interaction effect is not stronger than the FEDFUNDS change alone.
